annotations.label: Construction of naturals by succession
annotations.note: The naturals are the inductive type `Nat` built from zero and the successor constructor `Nat.succ`, defined in Lean core and used ubiquitously in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2 is the only even prime

anchor.snippet: the smallest and the only even prime number

annotations.note: `Nat.Prime.even_iff` states that a prime `p` is even iff `p = 2`, exactly capturing that 2 is the unique even prime.

annotations.label: 2 is the first superior highly composite number

anchor.snippet: the first superior highly composite number

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of superior highly composite numbers, so this fact is not formalized.

annotations.label: Even integer
annotations.note: `even_iff_two_dvd` proves `Even a ↔ 2 ∣ a`, equating evenness with divisibility by two.

annotations.label: Last digit of multiples of 2 in base 10

anchor.snippet: all multiples of 2 will end in

annotations.note: No declaration characterizes the base-10 last digit of even numbers, though base-b digits exist via `Nat.digits`.

annotations.label: Binary number system

anchor.snippet: Binary is a number system with a base of two

annotations.note: Binary representation is available via `Nat.bits`/`Nat.binaryRec` and `Nat.digits 2`, but there is no abstract definition of the binary number system itself.

annotations.label: Digon

anchor.snippet: A digon is a polygon with two sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general definition of polygon or digon.

annotations.label: Digons are degenerate in Euclidean space
annotations.note: With no notion of digon, this degeneracy statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-degenerate digons in spherical geometry
annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize spherical lunes/digons.

annotations.label: Two points determine a line

anchor.snippet: Two distinct points in a plane are always sufficient to define a unique line

annotations.note: Abstract incidence geometry provides `Configuration.HasLines.mkLine` (a line through any two points) plus a `Nondegenerate` axiom of at most one such line, but there is no Euclidean-plane theorem stating two points determine a unique line.

annotations.label: Field with two elements

anchor.snippet: The integers modulo 2 form the finite field

annotations.note: `ZMod.instField` gives a `Field (ZMod p)` instance for prime `p`; the case `p = 2` is the field with two elements.

annotations.label: Reduction modulo 2 records parity

anchor.snippet: reduction modulo 2 records the parity of an integer

annotations.note: `ZMod.intCast_zmod_eq_zero_iff_dvd a b` gives `(a : ZMod b) = 0 ↔ (b:ℤ) ∣ a`; specialized to `b = 2` this says the reduction is zero exactly for even integers, recording parity.

annotations.label: Characteristic 2 structures

anchor.snippet: structures of characteristic 2 have special behavior

annotations.note: Mathlib has a dedicated `CharTwo` namespace for `CharP R 2` rings with special lemmas such as `add_self_eq_zero`, `neg_eq`, and `sub_eq_add`.

annotations.label: Involution

anchor.snippet: A symmetry of order two is called an involution

annotations.note: `Function.Involutive f` is defined as `∀ x, f (f x) = x`, exactly a self-inverse map of order two.
